info.title: Phoenix Partner API
info.description:
The Phoenix Partner API provides integration endpoints for partner systems.
Use this documentation to explore available operations, authenticate via OAuth2, and test requests directly. Ben was ere.

get.summary: A ping endpoint that returns a pong message
get.description:
This endpoint allows you to ping the api and get back a pong message.
## Usage Example

```
GET /queries/ping
```

### Response

```json
{
  "reply": "pong"
}
```

## C# Client Example

```csharp
using var httpClient = new HttpClient();
httpClient.BaseAddress = new Uri("https://your-api-host");

var response = await httpClient.GetFromJsonAsync<PingResponse>(
    "/queries/ping");
Console.WriteLine(response.Reply); // Output: pong

public record PingResponse(string Reply);
```

## Python Client Example
```python
import requests

response = requests.get("https://your-api-host/queries/ping")
data = response.json()
print(data["reply"])  # Output: pong
```

get.summary: A sample endpoint that returns a greeting message and the current UTC timestamp
get.description:
This endpoint allows you to request a greeting message. It accepts an optional name parameter
and returns a greeting message along with the current UTC timestamp. It requires authorization and is

## Usage Example

```
GET /queries/sample?Name=Alice
```

### Response

```json
{
  "message": "Hello, Alice!",
  "timestampUtc": "2025-01-15T10:30:00+00:00"
}
```

If no name is provided, the response defaults to "Hello, world!".

## C# Client Example

```csharp
using var httpClient = new HttpClient();
httpClient.BaseAddress = new Uri("https://your-api-host");
httpClient.DefaultRequestHeaders.Authorization =
    new AuthenticationHeaderValue("Bearer", accessToken);

var response = await httpClient.GetFromJsonAsync<SampleResponse>(
    "/queries/sample?Name=Alice");

Console.WriteLine(response?.Message); // "Hello, Alice!"

public record SampleResponse(string Message, DateTimeOffset TimestampUtc);
```

## Python Client Example

```python
import requests

headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}"}
response = requests.get(
    "https://your-api-host/queries/sample",
    params={"Name": "Alice"},
    headers=headers,
)
data = response.json()
print(data["message"])        # Hello, Alice!
print(data["timestampUtc"])   # 2025-01-15T10:30:00+00:00
```

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the person to greet. If omitted, defaults to 'world'.

schema.description: The name of the person to greet. If omitted, defaults to 'world'.

reply.description: The reply message from the ping endpoint, this will be 'pong'

timestampUtc.description: The UTC timestamp when the response was generated
